title: Render + upload review
analysis: Build render review page (/review/render/{narrative_id}) with HTML5 video player and quality validation, upload status page (/review/uploads), video streaming endpoint, and supporting JSON APIs. The codebase has a mature pattern: review.py contains both /review/* page routes and /api/* JSON routes with HTMX-aware dual responses (HTML partial vs JSON). Templates extend base.html using Tailwind dark mode + HTMX. CatalogDB has edit_plans (narrative_id PK, edl_json, otio_path, validation_json, render_path) and uploads (narrative_id PK, youtube_video_id, youtube_url, uploaded_at, privacy_status) tables with single-record CRUD, but no list methods — need to add list_edit_plans() and list_uploads() with LEFT JOIN to narratives for title data. Video streaming is new to the codebase — Starlette's FileResponse supports Range headers natively for seeking. The existing gates API (/api/gates/{stage}/approve, /api/gates/{stage}/skip) handles render gate actions, so the template just wires to those endpoints. The narrative_scripts table (get_narrative_script) provides script scene data for the side-by-side view. The _REVIEW_LINKS dict in review_hub needs render/upload entries with pending counts.

prerequisites.description: Create tests/test_render_review.py with shared fixtures: db fixture (tmp_path file-backed CatalogDB), app fixture (create_app with init_default_gates), client fixture (TestClient), _seed_narrative helper, _seed_edit_plan helper (inserts narrative + upserts edit_plan with render_path and validation_json), _seed_upload helper (inserts narrative + upload record)

steps.description: Write failing tests for CatalogDB.list_edit_plans(): returns list of all edit plans joined with narrative title; returns empty list when none exist; includes all columns (narrative_id, edl_json, otio_path, validation_json, render_path, narrative_title)
Implement list_edit_plans() in CatalogDB — SELECT e.*, n.title as narrative_title FROM edit_plans e LEFT JOIN narratives n ON e.narrative_id = n.narrative_id. Returns list[dict].

Write failing tests for CatalogDB.list_uploads(): returns list of all uploads joined with narrative title; returns empty list when none exist; includes all columns (narrative_id, youtube_video_id, youtube_url, uploaded_at, privacy_status, narrative_title)

Implement list_uploads() in CatalogDB — SELECT u.*, n.title as narrative_title FROM uploads u LEFT JOIN narratives n ON u.narrative_id = n.narrative_id ORDER BY u.uploaded_at DESC. Returns list[dict].

Write failing tests for GET /api/renders/{narrative_id}: returns JSON with edit plan data (narrative_id, render_path, validation parsed from validation_json, narrative title); returns 404 for nonexistent narrative_id

Implement api_get_render route in review.py — GET /api/renders/{narrative_id}. Fetches edit_plan and narrative, parses validation_json into dict, returns composite JSON response with render status. Raises 404 if no edit plan found.

Write failing tests for GET /api/renders/{narrative_id}/video: returns 200 with video/mp4 content-type when render file exists (use tmp_path to create a small mock .mp4 file); returns 404 when no edit plan exists; returns 404 when render_path is null or file missing

Implement api_stream_video route in review.py — GET /api/renders/{narrative_id}/video. Fetches edit_plan, validates render_path exists on filesystem, returns FileResponse(render_path, media_type='video/mp4'). Starlette FileResponse handles Range headers natively for seeking.

Write failing tests for GET /api/uploads: returns JSON list of uploads with narrative_title joined; returns empty list when no uploads exist; includes all fields (narrative_id, youtube_video_id, youtube_url, uploaded_at, privacy_status, narrative_title)
Implement api_list_uploads route in review.py — GET /api/uploads. Calls db.list_uploads(), returns JSON list.

Write failing tests for GET /review/render/{narrative_id}: returns 200 HTML containing video player element when render_path exists and file is present; shows 'No render available' when render_path is null; shows validation results when validation_json is set; shows script scene list from narrative_scripts; returns 404 for nonexistent narrative; contains links to gate actions (approve, skip)

Implement render_review_page route (GET /review/render/{narrative_id}) in review.py and create renders.html template. Route fetches narrative, edit_plan, and narrative_script; parses validation_json and script_json; passes structured context to template. Template: extends base.html, two-column layout — left side has HTML5 <video> player pointing to /api/renders/{id}/video with controls, validation results as badges/list, and action buttons (Approve for Upload, Reject with notes textarea, Download link, Skip Upload); right side has script scene list parsed from narrative_scripts.

Write failing tests for GET /review/uploads: returns 200 HTML with table containing upload data (narrative title, YouTube video ID, YouTube URL, privacy status, uploaded_at); shows 'No uploads yet' when no uploads exist

Implement uploads_page route (GET /review/uploads) in review.py and create uploads.html template. Route calls db.list_uploads() and passes to template. Template: extends base.html, simple table with columns for narrative title (linked to render review), YouTube video ID, YouTube URL (as clickable link), privacy status badge, uploaded_at timestamp.

Write failing tests for review hub integration: when render gate is 'waiting', hub page contains 'render' card with link to /review/render and pending count of narratives with edit plans but no render_path; when upload gate is 'waiting', hub contains 'upload' card with link to /review/uploads

Update _REVIEW_LINKS dict to add 'render': '/review/render' and 'upload': '/review/uploads'. In review_hub route, add render stage pending count logic (count edit plans without render_path) and upload stage logic (count narratives in rendered status without upload record). Add simple GET /review/render index route listing narratives with edit plans and links to individual render review pages.

design_decisions.decision: Use Starlette FileResponse for video streaming rather than custom chunked streaming
design_decisions.rationale: Starlette's FileResponse natively supports HTTP Range headers for video seeking since v0.20+. It handles Content-Range, Accept-Ranges, and 206 Partial Content responses automatically. No need to implement custom byte-range parsing. This is the standard approach for serving static files in FastAPI/Starlette apps.

design_decisions.decision: Add list_edit_plans() and list_uploads() with LEFT JOIN to narratives table for title data
design_decisions.rationale: The uploads page needs narrative titles alongside upload data, and the hub needs edit plan counts. A LEFT JOIN is a single query vs N+1 individual lookups. Following the existing CatalogDB pattern of returning list[dict] from fetchall(). LEFT JOIN ensures records show even if narrative was deleted.

design_decisions.decision: Template render actions (approve, reject, skip) wire to existing gates API — no new action endpoints
design_decisions.rationale: The gates.py router already provides POST /api/gates/{stage}/approve, POST /api/gates/{stage}/skip, and PUT /api/gates/{stage} for updating status/notes. The render review template's HTMX buttons target these existing endpoints directly. This avoids duplicating gate logic and keeps the action surface consistent across all pipeline stages.

design_decisions.decision: Create a minimal GET /review/render index page listing narratives with edit plans
design_decisions.rationale: The review hub links to /review/render for the render gate, but individual render reviews are at /review/render/{id}. Without an index, the hub link would 404. A simple list page with links to individual render reviews is the minimal viable landing page. It also serves as a render queue overview.

design_decisions.decision: Parse validation_json and script_json in route handler, pass structured dicts to template
design_decisions.rationale: Following the existing pattern where _parse_narrative and _parse_cluster extract JSON fields in Python rather than doing JSON parsing in Jinja2 templates. Keeps templates simple and makes the data easy to test via JSON API endpoints.

Following the precedent set by test_cluster_review.py (task 47). Render review is a distinct feature area with its own fixtures (_seed_edit_plan, mock video files), DB method tests, and API tests. Separate files keep tests organized and allow parallel execution.

design_decisions.decision: Video streaming returns 404 when render_path is null or file doesn't exist on filesystem
design_decisions.rationale: A render may have an edit plan but no render_path yet (render not started), or the render_path may point to a file that was moved/deleted. Both cases should return 404 rather than 500, giving the client a clear signal that the video is not available.

reuse.how: Reuse directly for all new render/upload routes. Same file, no duplication. All new routes follow the try/finally db.close() pattern.

reuse.what: Existing gates API for render actions (approve, skip, reject with notes)
reuse.how: Render review template wires HTMX buttons directly to these existing endpoints. Approve → POST /api/gates/render/approve, Skip Upload → POST /api/gates/render/skip, Reject → PUT /api/gates/render with status='rejected' and notes from textarea.

reuse.what: CatalogDB existing single-record CRUD: get_edit_plan, get_upload, get_narrative, get_narrative_script
reuse.how: Render review route calls get_edit_plan + get_narrative + get_narrative_script to compose the full render review context. Video streaming route calls get_edit_plan to find render_path.

reuse.what: Template layout pattern: extends base.html, Tailwind dark mode, HTMX attributes
reuse.how: renders.html follows same structure: extends base.html, max-w-7xl container, title section, content grid. uploads.html follows the simpler table layout. Both use same Tailwind color scheme (surface-800, gray-400 text, colored badges).

reuse.what: Test fixture pattern: file-backed DB, create_app with init_default_gates, TestClient, _seed_* helpers
reuse.how: Create analogous fixtures in test_render_review.py: db fixture with tmp_path, app/client fixtures calling init_default_gates, _seed_edit_plan helper that inserts narrative + upserts edit plan, _seed_upload helper for upload records.
Create _parse_render helper that parses validation_json from edit_plan into a structured dict. Follows same pattern: pop JSON field, json.loads, add computed fields.

reuse.what: _REVIEW_LINKS dict and hub pending count pattern
reuse.how: Add 'render': '/review/render' and 'upload': '/review/uploads' entries. Add elif blocks for render/upload stages to compute pending counts: render counts edit plans without render_path, upload counts pending upload narratives.
